cards.title: Auto-Extend Appointments
cards.problem: ~9,300 clients will have schedule conflicts when Working Hours launches 5/5 because expired appointment series leave bookable ghost slots.
cards.metrics:
Primary: # clients without future appts. 9,300 → TBD.
Secondary: Bookings → 200/qtr
cards.solution: Auto-extend appointment series when client has 1 appt remaining; simplify directory visibility logic.

cards.title: Working Hours Scheduling
cards.problem: Availability is provider-elected and supply constrained; conflict management is entirely manual.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Avg provider slots per week → 10
Secondary: Bookings → 200/qtr
cards.solution: Providers set working hours; Octave auto-generates bookable availability from calendar whitespace and syncs busy blocks from Google Calendar.

cards.title: Prevent Double Bookings By Syncing Events to Google
cards.problem: Providers double-book with competitors because Octave appointments aren't visible on their Google Calendar.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Avg provider slots per week → 10
Secondary: Bookings → 200/qtr
cards.solution: Write Octave appointments and holds back to provider's Google Calendar as outbound events.

cards.title: Don't Delete and Incorporate Google Conflicts into Availability
cards.problem: 42% of providers forget to update availability and 30% of cancelled slots never resurface, causing silent supply loss.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Avg provider slots per week → 10
Secondary: Bookings → 200/qtr
cards.solution: Stop deleting availability when appointment is booked; slots remain open first-come-first-served.

cards.title: Multi Google Calendar Support
cards.problem: 20% of providers have multiple Google calendars but can only connect one, losing busy-block sync coverage.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Avg provider slots per week → 10
Secondary: Bookings → 200/qtr
cards.solution: Allow providers to connect up to 3 Google accounts × 3 calendars each for busy-block syncing.

cards.title: Provider Client List
cards.problem: Providers navigate multiple disconnected systems to find basic client info; no accurate centralized caseload view in Provider Portal.
cards.metrics: Primary: No-shows. Lagging indicator: Provider NPS.
cards.solution: Build comprehensive client list in Provider Portal showing active clients, new vs. established, insurance, measures pending, and quick links.

cards.title: Providers Can Self-Serve Support Questions
cards.problem: 5× increase in support response times after therapist EHR transition; Slack-based support creates bottlenecks, tracking gaps, and CCL burnout.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Provider NPS.
Secondary: Decrease ZD support tickets by 20%.
cards.solution: Implement in-app Zendesk live chat in Provider Portal (reuse Client Portal model); deprecate Slack as provider support channel.

cards.title: (WIP) Provider Daily Dashboard
cards.problem: Workflows spread across 4+ disparate systems; Daily Digest has stale data and <10% click rate despite >85% open rate.
cards.solution: Build real-time dashboard in Provider Portal with caseload insights, consolidated workflows, and actionable metrics to replace Daily Digest.

cards.title: Therapist Portal: Add Availability and Sync Google Calendar
cards.problem: High-friction slot creation (12 clicks per slot in therapist EHR); lack of unified therapist environment; supply constrained.
cards.metrics: Primary: Available slots per therapist (≥1 slot/wk). Secondary: Bookings. +5 slots/week, +30 bookings/week.
cards.solution: Build calendar view with personal and work events for easier availability management; predict 5 → 10 slots/adopted therapist/week.

cards.title: Ingest Partner Directory Data Into Therapist Database
cards.problem: Need to generate outbound roster of MSO partnership therapists to Partnerships as part of MSO partnership <> Embrace go-live; must scale to 8K therapists.
cards.metrics: Primary: Bookings with MSO partnership therapists from Partnerships directory. Initial volume is 10K therapists.
cards.solution: 1) Ingest MSO partnership CSV roster into therapist database; 2) Produce output file for Cigna directory.

cards.title: Pay Therapists on a Fixed Date Pay Schedule
cards.problem: Therapists don't know what day they'll get paid; bimonthly schedule with 3-day SLA creates inconsistency vs. competitors.
cards.metrics: Primary: % of therapists paid according to fixed schedule.
cards.solution: Roll out 16th/1st fixed pay schedule for all therapists; pay within 7 calendar days of pay period end.

cards.title: Therapist Onboarding: Operationalize Therapist Data
cards.problem: Onboarding spans 6+ systems and takes 47 days on average; 20% of therapists offboard before start date; visibility gaps throughout.
cards.metrics: Primary: Time to onboard (47 → <30 days target).
cards.solution: Bidirectional Tellescope ↔ therapist database sync; Tellescope as SSOT for therapist status/start date/bookable fields; retire Airtable as source of truth.

cards.title: Therapist Payments: v2
cards.problem: Confusing payment UI, sync delays, no fixed pay date, 15+ hrs manual intervention per pay period.
cards.metrics: A/R Days. Reduce ZD tickets by 50%; eliminate sync delays.
cards.solution: Integrate Wingspan payroll system for payment visibility; implement fixed biweekly pay schedule; automate invoice processing.

cards.title: Care Nav Operational Efficiency
cards.problem: CN SOPs have many manual, repeatable steps; workflows span 9+ tools causing task-switching inefficiency; no SSOT for client VOB data.
cards.solution: Automate CN workflows in Tellescope; reduce manual toil in matching, rematching, and consult processes.

cards.title: Launch Tellescope CRM for White-Glove Ops Teams
cards.problem: CN team used 7+ matching spreadsheets that didn't scale; Welkin lacked caseload view; no way to track client lifecycle through care.
cards.solution: Implement Tellescope as centralized CN CRM replacing MatchDash; continuous feature delivery for CN matching, rematching, and lifecycle workflows.

cards.title: Referrals Workstream — Simplify Partner Referral Workflow
cards.problem: No simple mechanism for partner PCPs to refer clients to Octave; referral process requires multiple manual steps across EHRs.
cards.solution: Enable Direct Secure Messaging (DSM) in Epic for partner systems (UCLA Health, etc.) to send referrals with a click.

cards.title: Therapist <> Staff Support Messaging
cards.problem: Google Workspace for therapists costs $240K/yr; therapists submit support via Gmail/Slack creating untrackable, unsecured requests.
cards.metrics: Eliminate Google Workspace cost ($400K savings); reduce Slack support load.
cards.solution: Build in-Portal secure messaging to Zendesk; staff respond via ZD; therapists notified via personal email.

cards.title: Quality and Clinical Excellence
cards.problem: QA feedback delayed up to 6 months; mail merge limited to 10–15 emails/batch; 3.75 hrs/therapist for audit review; limited audit scope.
cards.metrics: Quarterly review coverage for all therapists.
cards.solution: Build admin QA tool with auditor UI, automated feedback dissemination, and duplicate note detection.

cards.title: Multi-State Practice
cards.problem: Therapists licensed in multiple states can only practice in one, limiting supply in new markets and national payer attractiveness.
cards.metrics: Increase supply internally without increasing hiring budgets.
cards.solution: Configure our EHR to support multi-state availability, display, filtering, and claim processing for multi-state therapists.

cards.title: In-Person Appointments
cards.problem: 40%+ of therapy clients choose in-person care; Octave lacked infrastructure to support IRL scheduling, billing, and matching.
cards.solution: Capture therapist IRL intent/address in our EHR; support IRL availability, matching, encounter management, and IRL-specific claim processing.

cards.title: Therapist Data Revamp
cards.problem: Therapist data spread across Airtable and fragmented systems with no internal SSOT; operational brittleness and manual sync burden.
cards.solution: Build Therapist Data Service (therapist database) as the internal source of truth for therapist data, replacing Airtable and piecemeal tooling.
